extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won by timing his sprint perfectly from third wheel, overpowering VIVIANI despite DECEUNINCK's textbook leadout in the final 200 meters.
tactical_patterns: DECEUNINCK executed perfect leadout with MORKOV-RICHEZE-VIVIANI train but VIVIANI cracked when actually launching, exposing third-wheel positioning advantage.
Multiple sprint teams positioned wrong riders on key wheels in final kilometer causing aborted efforts, revealing chaos management separates winners.
ASTANA's entire squad congregated after FUGLSANG crash 16 miles out, demonstrating team staying-together protocol can sacrifice race positioning.

rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: abandoned while 8th overall after second major crash, suggesting accumulated damage cost him podium contention
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: had perfect third-wheel position from own leadout but lacked power to hold off EWAN, signaling form decline
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: demonstrated superior sprint timing judgment by waiting behind DECEUNINCK train then accelerating with more power in final 200m
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: crashed early clipping curb/teammate, minimal team concern suggests fatigue errors creeping in but no abandonment risk
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: fourth place earned massive green jersey points haul despite not contesting win, tactical points-maximization over stage hunting
